rulings.Case Title: ISABELLA SERRANO VS. BENJAMIN FINNESTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File An Amendment To The Answer Of Defendant Lanny Jay
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 28, 2025, line 4. Defendant Jay's Motion for Leave to File an Amendment to Answer is DENIED. The lack of a certificate of occupancy is not a valid defense against a tenant's habitability or harassment claims, accordingly the amendment would be futile. The defense is also subsumed in the "In Pari Delicto" defense asserted in the existing answer. Defendant's proposed defense regarding SFRO 37.9(f) is not an affirmative defense as phrased, as it does not bring in a "new matter that is not responsive to the essential allegations in the plaintiff's complaint." (The Bank of New York Mellon v. Preciado (2013) 224 Cal.App.4th Supp. 1, 8.) It is also already subsumed in the "failure to state a claim" defense asserted in the existing answer. Defendant's request to amend their Answer to include attorney's fees is denied. Defendant may seek attorney fees after judgment is authorized by law. Amending the Answer is unnecessary. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: 1000 VAN NESS LP VS. 5910 INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 28, 2025, line 6. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff filed their complaint on February 13, 2025 against 5910 Investments LLC "in its capacity as Performing Parcel Owner." (Complaint, para. 5.) On April 24 and again on June 16, 2025, this Court in Case No. CGC-24-620014, 5910 Investments LLC v. 1000 Van Ness LP, granted Plaintiff's Motion for Receivership, and suspended 1000 Van Ness LP's rights and powers as Performing Parcel Owner. On June 9, and again on June 16, 2025, the Court continued the demurrer for supplemental briefing on the effect, if any, the order appointing a receiver would have on this case, and ordered the parties to brief the issue of standing and whether 1000 Van Ness LP is able to maintain the suit as is. Plaintiff does not dispute 1000 Van Ness LP no longer has standing to pursue this lawsuit "in its capacity as Performing Parcel Owner," but claims that it may bring its claims related to the Parking Garage, in its capacity as an individual parcel owner. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to substitute the real party in interest. (Cloud v. Northrop Grumman Corp. (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 995, 1004, 1011.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: INTEGRAL SENIOR LIVING MANAGEMENT, LLC ET AL VS. WILLIAM ATHERLEY
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 28, 2025, line 10. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part, subject to opposition. Summary judgment is denied, insofar as plaintiffs request inclusion of monetary damages in the judgment. Plaintiffs' asserted daily rental value is based upon defendant's current service fee, which includes fees for services that are personal to the defendant's needs. Plaintiffs have not offered any authority for their apparent argument that these amounts which are in part personal to defendant are the reasonable rental value of the premises. Summary adjudication of plaintiffs' right to recover possession of the premises is granted, subject to opposition. Summary adjudication of plaintiffs' right to recover unlawful detainer damages is denied, for the reasons previously stated. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: DECLAN TREANOR VS. MICHELLE SANCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 28, 2025, line 11. Plaintiff's Motion for an Order Deeming Admitted Truth of Facts is DENIED. The statements in the declaration and the proof of service of the underlying discovery regarding the date and method of service of the underlying discovery appear to be false, as they are inconsistent with the information shown on the USPS tracking printout. Having no reliable evidence of the date and method of service of the discovery in question, plaintiff has not shown that defendant Olivas has failed to serve a timely response. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LP, A CALIFORNIA VS. BOLDBAYER PUREV ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 28, 2025, line 12. Defendant's Motion to Strike Complaint is DENIED, 5 days to answer. The verification of the Complaint is valid on its face. The verification states that the matters are true of the declarant's own knowledge, except as to those matters stated on information and belief. The form verification page indicates that it is required that any items stated on information and belief be identified. Therefore, since no items are identified as being stated on information and belief, the plain meaning of this verification is that all of the items in the verified pleading are true of the declarant's own knowledge. Defendant's interpretation of the form verification page would make it impossible to use this form if there are not any items stated on information and belief. Defendant provides no support for the implicit proposition that it is improper to use this form verification page if the entire pleading is verified based on personal knowledge. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
